Description: Uncovered a JSON Web Token, which may lead to unauthorized access to web applications and sensitive user data.
Author: lxrzlyr
Message:
feat(v3.4): AI IDE microservice (code_intel/LSP) + preset MCP catalog + failure triage + turn rail

Implements the full S1-v3.4 plan. DeepAgent stays control-plane only —
enhances opencode's default agent/runtime/session/tool/MCP, never rebuilds them.

LSP → AI IDE microservice (L1-L6):
- L1 extended LSP.Service: typeDefinition/typeHierarchy/inlayHint/codeAction/
  executeCommand/rename(read-only preview)/documentHighlight/foldingRange +
  capability probe; completion/signatureHelp kept but out of the agent intent set.
- L2 code_intel: symbol-driven entry (resolveSymbol -> coords), disambiguation,
  position fallback, graceful no-server grep hint.
- L3 overview orchestration + bounded relation expansion (calls/inherits) w/ cycle
  detection; L4 cross-file + workspace diagnostics pull, high+ plan-stale latch.
- L5 snippet rendering + evidence artifact (evidence_kind on gateway schema).
- L6 code_intel promoted to default-visible (codeIntelTool flag), grep guidance.

Preset MCP catalog + security (M1-M7, M2 provenance fix):
- M2: explicit tool provenance via WeakMap (no more name.includes(":") guessing);
  registry projection carries it; request.ts maps to each exit's token.
- M1 catalog: 6 vetted entries, default not connected, one-click enable.
- M7 SECURITY: tier->permission gate is derived at runtime by structurally matching
  the live config against catalog templates (McpCatalog.deriveTier), NOT from the
  attacker-writable persisted riskTier — read_only auto-allow cannot be forged.
  mcpReadOnlyAutoAllow flag (default on) restores always-ask. Read-only SQL guard
  (recursive arg extraction) as defense-in-depth atop --access-mode=restricted.
  add endpoint strips client riskTier. Credential secure-storage indirection
  deferred to V3.5 (documented).

Failure triage (T1-T3, independent line):
- T1 ValidationResult.exit_code passthrough + changedThisRound signal.
- T2 classifyFailure() three-tier (red>yellow>green) w/ 4 yellow substates.
- T3 loop routing + round_control.action advance vs terminal channels.

U-NAV turn rail (frontend): left-edge fisheye segmented scale, hover preview,
click-to-jump, active-turn sync; pure app/ui, zero backend change.

Verification: four-package typecheck green; test/mcp 103 pass, core
test/deepagent 348 pass; SDK regenerated. Excludes an unrelated repo-wide
markdown/JSON/mdx formatter sweep and docs/ (kept local-only).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Uncovered a JSON Web Token, which may lead to unauthorized access to web applications and sensitive user data.
Author: lxrzlyr
Message:
fix(v3.3): no-reply crash from custom-tool load + sweep upstream fork leftovers

Root cause of the silent no-reply: the tool registry dynamically imports every
file in `.deepagent-code/tool/`, and a failed import surfaced as a Die defect
inside prompt_async, aborting the whole turn before the model was called. The
trigger was leftover opencode sample tools importing `@deepagent-code/plugin`,
whose source used `.js` specifiers that Node (the Electron sidecar runtime)
cannot resolve to `.ts` — Bun could, so `bun dev` masked it.

Fixes:
- registry: wrap the per-file dynamic import in catch+skip+log so one broken
  custom tool can never crash a prompt again (regression test added).
- plugin: switch source relative specifiers .js -> .ts and enable
  allowImportingTsExtensions + rewriteRelativeImportExtensions so it resolves
  under Node and Bun at runtime while the build still emits .js to dist.
- json-schema: thread `options` through normalize() recursion so stripNull
  survives re-entry (nested optional(NullOr(x)) no longer leaks type:null).
- plan-write: drop unnecessary NullOr from tool Parameters.

Upstream fork-leftover cleanup:
- remove dead opencode maintainer automation: github-triage / github-pr-search
  tools + their triage / duplicate-pr agents + triage.yml workflow +
  script/duplicate-pr.ts; drop the check-duplicates job from pr-management.yml
  (keep add-contributor-label).
- rename orphaned .deepagent-code/opencode.jsonc -> deepagent-code.jsonc so the
  project config is actually read (loader expects the deepagent-code name).
- fix dangling tui-vim.tsx reference in specs/tui-plugins.md.

Note: also captures pre-existing uncommitted branch WIP (plan-system,
domain-pack, self-learning) per a full working-tree commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: lxrzlyr
Message:
DeepAgent Code: initial release

First commit of DeepAgent Code, derived from opencode (MIT). This is a curated
minimal snapshot sufficient to build and package the desktop app via CI; it
intentionally omits docs/, agent config dirs, specs/, README translations, and
the upstream publish/release workflows.

Includes:
- Full opencode -> deepagent-code rebrand across all packages (names, imports,
  app identity, protocol scheme, data dir, server port — isolated from any
  opencode install so the two coexist).
- DeepAgent V3 control plane in packages/core (domain packs, durable knowledge
  store, activation/round/diagnosis, ship gate).
- MIT compliance: LICENSE retains the opencode copyright and adds DeepAgent
  Code; NOTICE.md; an About tab in settings surfacing the full license.
- Desktop packaging: optional macOS signing/notarization (auto-detected; falls
  back to unsigned) and a lean .github/workflows/desktop-build.yml that builds
  and uploads installers as artifacts without publishing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Discovered a potential authorization token provided in a curl command header, which could compromise the curl accessed resource.
Match:
curl cURL
curl -X GET '/api/endpoint' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer REDACTED'
